nameMeanings.name: Brocton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Broxton in Cheshire, an obscure name, possibly from Old English burgæsn ‘burial place’.
habitational name from Browston in Suffolk, recorded in Domesday Book as Brockestuna, from the Old English personal name Brocc (from Old English brocc ‘badger’) + Old English tūn ‘settlement’.

variants.name: Broxson
meanings: English (Staffordshire): variant of   Broxton  . This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Brocton was born about 1391, in England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with Sir William Brocton Sr. She died in Stanton Long, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Brocton(은)는 대략 1391년 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir William Brocton Sr(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 스탠턴 론그에서 사망했다.
